note_data.title: Seerat-un-Nabi (PBUH)
keyPoints: Prophet Muhammad (PBUH) was born in Makkah in 571 CE in the noble tribe of Quraish
His father Abdullah died before his birth and mother Aminah died when he was 6 years old
He was raised by his grandfather Abdul Muttalib and later by his uncle Abu Talib
Before Prophethood, he was known as 'As-Sadiq' (The Truthful) and 'Al-Ameen' (The Trustworthy)
He received first revelation (Wahi) in Cave Hira at the age of 40 through Angel Jibreel (AS)
The Hijra (Migration) from Makkah to Madinah took place in 622 CE, marking the beginning of Islamic calendar
He established the first Islamic state in Madinah with Constitution of Madinah (Misaq-e-Madinah)
The Treaty of Hudaybiyyah was signed in 6 AH which led to peaceful spread of Islam
Conquest of Makkah (Fatah Makkah) occurred in 8 AH without bloodshed, showing his merciful nature
He passed away in 11 AH in Madinah at the age of 63, completing the message of Islam

definitions.definition: The biography, life history and conduct of Prophet Muhammad (PBUH) which serves as a perfect model for all mankind
Divine revelation sent by Allah to His Prophets through Angel Jibreel to guide humanity
The migration of Prophet Muhammad (PBUH) and his companions from Makkah to Madinah in 622 CE
The Constitution of Madinah which established the first Islamic state and defined rights of Muslims, Jews and other communities
The perfect example and ideal model of conduct set by Prophet Muhammad (PBUH) for all Muslims to follow

importantQuestions.question: What is the importance of studying Seerat-un-Nabi (PBUH)?
importantQuestions.answer: Studying Seerat helps us understand the perfect character of Prophet (PBUH), provides guidance for all aspects of life, and shows us the practical implementation of Islamic teachings in daily life.

importantQuestions.question: Describe the early life of Prophet Muhammad (PBUH) before Prophethood.
importantQuestions.answer: Prophet Muhammad (PBUH) was born in Makkah in 571 CE. His father Abdullah died before his birth. His mother Aminah died when he was 6 years old. He was first cared for by his grandfather Abdul Muttalib and after his death by uncle Abu Talib. He worked as a shepherd and later as a trader. He was known for his honesty and trustworthiness, earning titles As-Sadiq and Al-Ameen. He married Hazrat Khadijah (RA) at age 25. He used to meditate in Cave Hira and received first revelation at age 40.

mcqs.question: At what age did Prophet Muhammad (PBUH) receive the first revelation?
In which year did the Hijra take place?
What was the name of the first wife of Prophet Muhammad (PBUH)?

commonMistakes: Students often confuse the dates of birth and death of Prophet (PBUH)
Many students mix up the sequence of events in Makkah and Madinah periods
Students sometimes forget to write (PBUH) after Prophet's name which loses marks
Confusion between Hijri and Christian calendar dates is common
Students often miss mentioning the titles As-Sadiq and Al-Ameen when writing about pre-Prophethood period
examTips: This chapter usually has 2-3 MCQs and at least one long question in BSEK papers
Always memorize exact dates in both Hijri and Christian calendars
Practice writing the chronological sequence of major events
Remember to use respectful titles (PBUH), (AS), (RA) throughout your answers
Focus on the moral lessons from each incident in Seerat for better marks
note_data.keyDiagram: Timeline diagram showing major events in Prophet's life: Birth (571 CE), First Revelation (610 CE), Hijra (622 CE/1 AH), Treaty of Hudaybiyyah (6 AH), Conquest of Makkah (8 AH), Death (11 AH/632 CE)
